program_name: Geophysics for Earth and Climate Processes, Risks and Resources

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification with a minimum three-year undergraduate background and proven preparation in quantitative or Earth-science-related fields. The official application page specifically mentions Geology, Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, and Computer Sciences. Country-based entry-title and GPA rules also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) is required, with proven skills in disciplines such as Geology, Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, and Computer Sciences.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Applicants must verify country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements on the official University page linked from the application platform. No separate programme-wide GPA threshold was stated on the reviewed 2026/27 programme pages.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if the previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official 2026/27 application page restricts Call Three to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Provide a colour scanned PDF. An English or Italian translation is required if needed. No legalised documents are required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.
The University instructs applicants to check the chosen programme for any additional required document.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript must certify passed exams and grades; where possible it should also show credits and course syllabus information.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly by 15 July and apply for the study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants who are in Italy at enrolment stage.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard written entry test published
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The reviewed 2026/27 official pages describe admission through verification of entry requirements and application review, but do not publish a separate written test or interview for this programme.

source_quotes.quote: Access: open access with admission requirements
proven skills in disciplines such as Geology, Physics, Engineering, Mathematics and Computer Sciences
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)

uncertainty_notes: The 2026/27 programme listing uses the new title 'Geophysics for Earth and Climate Processes, Risks and Resources', while the reviewed application and department pages still describe the award as 'Geophysics for Natural Risks and Resources'.

The department page snippet available through official search results reports the class as LM-79, while the 2025 educational-offer listing reports LM-79 R; this JSON records the 2026/27 educational-offer class format.
